mutations.change: Convert absent or transparent patch texels into opaque palette-zero samples before masked and sprite composition.
mutations.reason: The grate and asymmetric billboard fixtures contain holes whose underlying world pixel must remain unchanged.

mutations.change: Add one hundred eighty degrees to viewer-relative object bearing before selecting the directional sprite rotation.
mutations.reason: Eight compass probes independently freeze every rotation bin and its half-sector boundary behavior.

mutations.change: Always select the rotation-zero lump even for a state explicitly declaring all eight directional rotations.
mutations.reason: The asymmetric directional fixture requires distinct resolved assets and mirror flags around the object.

mutations.change: Select a dual-name sprite lump but suppress the recorded horizontal flip for its second encoded frame and rotation.
mutations.reason: An asymmetric three-column patch makes mirrored and unmirrored samples differ at exact spot pixels.

mutations.change: Retain projected billboard samples outside the canonical screen column and row ranges instead of clipping them.
mutations.reason: A partially offscreen purpose pose exposes candidate bounds while forbidding out-of-range winning pixels.

mutations.change: Mark every projected sample sector-visible without consulting its active per-column portal clip interval.
mutations.reason: The narrow sector-window scene retains only one independently identified billboard texel.

mutations.change: Allow sprites behind the nearest solid wall depth to participate in overlay winner selection at covered pixels.
mutations.reason: The wall-occlusion scene contains a valid projected sprite whose complete visible result must be empty.

mutations.change: Treat sprite depth equal to solid wall depth as visible instead of applying the fixed strict epsilon comparison.
mutations.reason: An equality boundary probe freezes the wall-first policy without early depth rounding.

mutations.change: Reverse depth ordering so a farther eligible sprite wins over a nearer sprite on every overlapping screen pixel.
mutations.reason: The two-depth scene independently requires all shared covered pixels to select the nearer stable object.

mutations.change: Resolve equal-depth ties with sprite candidates ahead of masked middle texture candidates instead of the fixed class order.
mutations.reason: A masked texel and two sprites deliberately share one pixel and one exact analytic depth.

mutations.change: Reverse stable object id ordering among same-class sprite candidates at identical depth and screen pixel.
mutations.reason: Two otherwise identical sprites with ids four and five overlap and require the lower stable id.

mutations.change: Remove one renderable monster state from the independently traversed E1M1 world-state sprite closure.
mutations.reason: The closure assertion compares every reachable state and rotation against seeded sprite asset names.

mutations.change: Remove projectile rows from the renderer world-sprite catalog even though engine actions can spawn them.
mutations.reason: The fixed catalog contract requires decoration, pickup, monster, projectile, and effect classifications.

mutations.change: Round sprite and masked analytic depth before wall clipping and stable per-pixel ordering.
mutations.reason: Near-equality wall and sprite probes differ below one unit and inherit the renderer numeric tolerance.

mutations.change: Loop over live objects or pixels in PL/SQL rather than projecting and ranking candidates with relational SQL.
mutations.reason: The charter prohibits procedural shadow rendering loops and assigns projection and composition decisions to SQL.

mutations.change: Render immutable map things directly while ignoring current session-bound mobj state, position, angle, and visibility.
mutations.reason: Diagnostic poses and later simulation require current relational object state rather than spawn-only map rows.

mutations.change: Sample two-sided middle textures without their sidedef horizontal and vertical offsets or floor-mod wrapping.
mutations.reason: The masked mini-scene freezes shifted opaque and transparent samples including a negative offset case.

mutations.change: Embed E1M1 diagnostic coordinates, expected hashes, evaluator paths, or caller markers in production SQL.
mutations.reason: Static audit rejects fixture coupling while transformed and held-back poses require general bound behavior.
